mapper.select: select id, tenant_id as tenantId, calendar_code as calendarCode, description, timezone as timezone, catch_up_policy as catchUpPolicy, cutoff_time as cutoffTime, late_arrival_tolerance_min as lateArrivalToleranceMin, sla_offset_min as slaOffsetMin from batch.business_calendar where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and calendar_code = #{calendarCode} and enabled = true order by id desc limit 1

select id, tenant_id as "tenantId", calendar_code as "calendarCode", calendar_name as "calendarName", timezone, holiday_roll_rule as "holidayRollRule", catch_up_policy as "catchUpPolicy", catch_up_max_days as "catchUpMaxDays", enabled, description, created_at as "createdAt", updated_at as "updatedAt" from batch.business_calendar where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and id = #{id}
mapper.insert: insert into batch.business_calendar ( tenant_id, calendar_code, calendar_name, timezone, holiday_roll_rule, catch_up_policy, catch_up_max_days, enabled, description ) values ( #{tenantId}, #{calendarCode}, #{calendarName}, #{timezone}, #{holidayRollRule}'SKIP', #{catchUpPolicy}'NONE', #{catchUpMaxDays}0, #{enabled}true, #{description} ) on conflict (tenant_id, calendar_code) do update set calendar_name = excluded.calendar_name, timezone = excluded.timezone, holiday_roll_rule = excluded.holiday_roll_rule, catch_up_policy = excluded.catch_up_policy, catch_up_max_days = excluded.catch_up_max_days, enabled = excluded.enabled, description = excluded.description, updated_at = current_timestamp
mapper.update: update batch.business_calendar set calendar_name = #{calendarName}, timezone = #{timezone}, holiday_roll_rule = #{holidayRollRule}, catch_up_policy = #{catchUpPolicy}, catch_up_max_days = #{catchUpMaxDays}, description = #{description}, updated_at = current_timestamp where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and id = #{id}
